title: Per-Origin Politeness, Token Timing, Retry Budgets, and Fairness · Language Lens: Retryability
summary: A shared ticker spaces starts globally, one origin monopolizes slots, Retry-After is ignored, jitter is unbounded, and transient failures retry forever. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go retryability increment in monotonic per-origin eligibility scheduler. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-ca…
objectives: Classify transport and HTTP failures by method safety, response evidence, attempt, elapsed, and crawl budgets.
Enforce minimum spacing between request starts with monotonic time and avoid holding shared locks while sleeping.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
